export_requirements.export_permit: No CFIA export permit required for personal pet exports. Some destination countries may require CFIA endorsement of health certificate.

import_requirements.microchip: Required (ISO 11784/11785). Must be implanted before or at same time as rabies vaccination
import_requirements.rabies_vaccination: Required. Minimum age: 12 weeks. 21-day wait after vaccination before travel. Standard EU protocol. 21-day wait from first vaccination.
import_requirements.titre_test: Required for: Pets from unlisted third countries. Not required for: Pets from EU and listed third countries (all P1 countries listed). Minimum: 0.5 IU/ml. 90-day wait after blood draw
import_requirements.quarantine: No routine quarantine. Penalty quarantine up to varies days if non-compliant
import_requirements.import_permit: No formal import permit. EU pet passport or AHC serves as documentation.

airlines.name: American Airlines
airlines.policy_summary: Small pets allowed in cabin. Cargo: American Airlines Cargo handles pet transport

airlines.name: Air Canada
airlines.policy_summary: Small pets allowed in cabin. Cargo: Air Canada Cargo handles pet transport in heated/pressurized hold. Brachycephalic breeds restricted from cargo year-round

timeline_steps.timing: Before any vaccinations. Must be done first.
At least 21 days before travel (after microchip)
Blood drawn 30+ days after vaccination. 90-day wait after successful result.
Within 10 days of travel (check destination-specific requirements)

timeline_steps.action: Book IATA-compliant flight and arrange pet cargo/cabin booking
timeline_steps.timing: 2-4 weeks before travel. Confirm pet space availability.

timeline_steps.action: Travel day: arrive early, present all documentation
timeline_steps.timing: 3-4 hours before departure for cargo pets

cost_factors: Airline cargo/cabin fees (varies by carrier, pet weight, and crate size)
Pet transport agent fee (if using a relocation service)
key_warnings: Brachycephalic (flat-faced) breeds face cargo restrictions on most airlines. Check breed-specific policies before booking.
Microchip MUST be implanted before rabies vaccination. Vaccination given before microchip is invalid and the clock restarts.

seo.title: Pet Transport from Canada to France | Complete Guide
seo.description: How to transport your dog or cat from Canada to France. Import requirements, airline options, costs, and step-by-step process.

content.h1: Pet Transport from Canada to France
content.overview:
Moving your pet from Canada to France takes planning.

Expect to start preparations 6-10 weeks before your travel date. A rabies titre test (blood test) is required, which means an additional waiting period after vaccination. Airlines serving this route include United Airlines, American Airlines, and Air Canada.

Getting the paperwork right is the hardest part. Once that's sorted, the flight itself is the easy bit.

sections.heading: Key things to know about this route
sections.body: Compared to some international routes, Canada to France is relatively straightforward. That doesn't mean you can leave it to the last minute.

sections.heading: Things we'd tell a friend moving their pet to France
sections.body:
Start the microchip and vaccination process as early as possible. The biggest mistake people make is leaving it too late. Once you miss a deadline, the entire timeline pushes back.

Some airlines on this route (United Airlines, American Airlines) allow small pets in the cabin. Your pet must fit in a carrier under the seat. Weight limits are strict: usually 8-10kg including the carrier.

Use a pet transport agent if you're not confident handling the paperwork yourself. A good agent knows the current rules, has relationships with airline cargo departments, and can troubleshoot problems before they become disasters.

sections.heading: What happens on travel day
sections.body:
Arrive at the airport at least 3 hours before departure if your pet is travelling as cargo. You'll need to present all original documents: health certificate, vaccination records, microchip number, and any import permits.

Your pet's crate must be clean, labelled with your contact details and the consignee's details at the destination, and have food and water containers attached. Live animal stickers and orientation arrows are required.

On arrival in France, you (or your agent) collect your pet from the cargo terminal. There may be an inspection by veterinary authorities at the airport. Have copies of all paperwork ready.

faqs.question: Will my pet need to go into quarantine when arriving in France?
faqs.answer: No, not if all your paperwork is correct. France does not require routine quarantine for pets with valid documentation. However, if anything is missing or incorrect, your pet could face penalty quarantine at your expense. Getting the paperwork right is not optional.

faqs.question: Can my pet fly in the cabin from Canada to France?
faqs.answer: Some airlines allow small pets (typically under 8kg with carrier) in the cabin. United Airlines, American Airlines offer cabin options on this route. Larger pets must travel as cargo. Airlines with cargo service include United Airlines, American Airlines, Air Canada. Availability varies by season and aircraft type, so confirm when booking.

faqs.question: How far in advance should I start preparing to move my pet from Canada to France?
faqs.answer: Start at least 6-10 weeks before your planned travel date. The process begins with microchipping, then rabies vaccination (with a mandatory waiting period), then any required blood tests, health certificates, and permit applications. Rushing the timeline is the most common reason people hit problems.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to transport a dog from Canada to France?
faqs.answer: The total cost depends on your dog's size, the airline, and whether you use a transport agent. For a medium-sized dog, expect to pay for: veterinary fees (vaccinations, health certificate, microchip), an IATA-compliant crate, airline cargo fees, government endorsements, and any import permits or quarantine fees at the destination. Using a door-to-door agent adds to the cost but handles the complexity for you.

faqs.question: What documents does my pet need to travel from Canada to France?
faqs.answer: Your pet will need: proof of ISO microchip, rabies vaccination certificate, rabies titre test results, official health certificate. The health certificate must be issued by an official veterinarian within 10 days of travel. Some documents need government endorsement. Check every requirement against the current rules, because regulations change and an outdated document can mean your pet doesn't fly.

faqs.question: Can I fly my French Bulldog or Pug from Canada to France?
faqs.answer: Brachycephalic (flat-faced) breeds face restrictions on most airlines, particularly for cargo transport. These breeds are more vulnerable to breathing difficulties at altitude and in heat. Many airlines impose seasonal embargoes (typically summer months), and some restrict flat-faced breeds from cargo year-round. A few airlines still accept them with additional veterinary clearance. Check the specific airline's current policy before booking.
